name: boss_rugby
description: Final boss rugby player, 3/4 top-down (Zelda-like), south-facing. Blue jersey with a shamrock over the heart, rugby ball under the left arm. Original pixel art. Engine mirrors horizontally as needed.

hitbox.note: body only; excludes ball and extended limbs

idle.note: Breathing. Feet planted, upper body rises 1px.

run.note: 0 = left knee up, 1 = pass, 2 = right knee up, 3 = pass. Ball stays clamped under the left arm.

tackle_charge.note: Charge-up telegraph BEFORE the tackle dash. Deep crouch, head sunk, frown; right boot paws the ground like a bull (scrape + dust), body trembles 1px, snort puffs alternate sides. Loop ~0.8-1.2s while aiming, then play tackle. No hitbox during this phase.

tackle.note: 0 = windup crouch (play once), then loop 1-2 while charging. Head down, open hand reaching. Use as the charge attack; deal contact damage. Telegraph first with tackle_charge.

spawnProjectile.note: Spawn ball_spin projectile at start of frame 2 (sole contact), frame px from top-left. Fires toward the player; engine sets direction.

kick.note: 0 = holds ball in front, 1 = ball drop + knee cocked, 2 = CONTACT (sole toward camera), 3 = follow-through. Return to idle after.

dropBall.note: The boss no longer holds the ball in any defeat frame. Optionally spawn a ball entity (ball_spin frame 0) here and let it roll away.

defeat.note: 0 = slump (eyes closed), 1 = falls to knees, 2 = collapsed. No loop, hold frame 2.

pivot.note: bottom-center, same ground line as the boss

spawn.offset: 4-8 px behind the boss pivot, opposite the travel direction

tackle_dust.note: Dust trail for the tackle dash. Play once and destroy: puff -> billow -> break into clumps -> fade. No outline (soft effect), no hitbox. Purely cosmetic.

projectile.name: ball_spin
projectile.note: End-over-end tumbling rugby ball, 45deg per frame (4 frames = half turn; the oval is symmetric so it loops seamlessly). Horizontal projectile; rotate/mirror per travel direction in-engine.
